title: Bronchiectasis
chief_complaint: I've had a chronic cough producing thick green sputum for years. It's getting worse. I cough up about half a cup of phlegm every morning. I've had recurrent pneumonias.

physical_exam.Lungs: Coarse crackles at both bases, worse on right. Wheezing on forced expiration. Digital clubbing.
physical_exam.Chest: Increased AP diameter. Use of accessory muscles.

hidden_data.ct_chest: Cylindrical bronchiectasis in right middle and lower lobes and lingula. Bronchial wall thickening. Signet ring sign (dilated bronchus > adjacent pulmonary artery). Mucus plugging.
hidden_data.cxr: Tram-track opacities in right lower lobe. Ring shadows.
hidden_data.sputum: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (heavy growth). Sensitive to Ciprofloxacin and Piperacillin-Tazobactam.
hidden_data.immunoglobulins: Normal IgG, IgA, IgM. IgE 250 (slightly elevated).

correct_diagnosis: Bronchiectasis with Chronic Pseudomonas aeruginosa Colonization
key_learning_points: Bronchiectasis = permanent, irreversible dilatation of bronchi from chronic infection/inflammation cycle
CT hallmark: Signet ring sign (bronchus > adjacent artery)
Common causes: Post-infectious (TB, pneumonia), CF, immunodeficiency, allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis (ABPA), autoimmune, ciliary dysfunction
Chronic Pseudomonas colonization = worse prognosis, accelerated lung function decline

Treatment: Airway clearance (chest physiotherapy, oscillatory devices), mucolytics, bronchodilators, inhaled antibiotics (Tobramycin, Colistin) for chronic Pseudomonas, rotating oral antibiotics for exacerbations
